meta.title: Deeptech Attractiveness Index — Winsorized (95th percentile)
meta.description: Composite index benchmarking 26 countries (15 European, 11 African) as deeptech R&D destinations, using winsorized scores.
meta.methodology: OECD/JRC Handbook (2008). 6 equal-weighted pillars; arithmetic mean of z-standardized indicators. Robustness treatment: the 12 per-capita count indicators are winsorized at the 95th percentile (upper tail, GII practice) and re-standardized.
meta.scoreVariant: Winsorized (95th) + Policy sub-pillars re-weighted 3:1 (Policy Quality 0.75 / Targeted Legislation 0.25)
notes: composite.score and composite.rankOverall are the AUTHORITATIVE winsorized values from the Robustness sheet (Wins score / Wins rank).

Pillar and indicator winsorized scores are reconstructed via the validated winsorize-then-restandardize method (equivalent to winsorizing raw values); mean abs deviation 0.007 (max 0.018) vs a full recompute of the composite.
Consequently composite.score may differ very slightly from the arithmetic mean of the six displayed pillar scores.
Financial Capacity and Policy pillars contain no count indicators and are identical to the base index.
8 indicator cells remain missing (z = null) and are not imputed; render as n/a.
GDP is not in the source workbooks; gdpUsd is null pending a verified source.

CORRECTION: Output 'Scientific Translation' sub-pillar now half-weights resident patents and PCT (0.25 each) and full-weights citable documents (0.50), per V2_Weights and Section 3.7.2 (patents-PCT correlate r=0.97). Previously these three were equal-weighted. Output pillar score and ranks updated accordingly; all other pillars, indicator z-values, and the authoritative composite scores/ranks are unchanged.

RE-WEIGHT: Mission-Oriented Policy sub-pillars changed from 0.5/0.5 to 0.75/0.25 (Policy Quality : Targeted Legislation) so the single Startup-Act indicator no longer holds half the pillar. composite.score and all ranks are RECOMPUTED under this weighting and therefore supersede the workbook's 1:1 'Wins score'. Other five pillars and all indicator z-values unchanged.
meta.rankVsBase: Main shift vs base ranking: Estonia 3 -> 7; Kenya/South Africa swap to 19/18; Spearman 0.992 vs base.

pillars.name: Human Capital
pillars.fullName: Scientific Knowledge and Advanced Human Capital
